hijacking things that Dan talked about yesterday. So here is how you apply it either in person or in copy. And again, I'm using just the financial advisors example here. You know, Mr. Prospect, although your portfolio might not be the largest I've ever seen, and that's an important line. Because when you're doing the ego stroke, people aren't stupid. Your prospect isn't stupid. And so when you go way over the top using that strategy, it comes across as manipulative. And a tactic known as a tactic blown. So we want to be a little bit more subtle. So by saying, hey, your portfolio might not be the largest I've ever seen, that's an important thing. So you want to make a, you're going to give them an ego stroke, but you want to make it sound realistic so it sounds real to them. So it sounds like it's a real compliment. It's much more impressive than most, and you definitely have planned for your future better than most of my clients. So again, the important part of that is when you're going to do that strategy that Dan talked about over the weekend, or a couple days ago, it's important that you add in that little disclaimer, that little takeaway. And then one of my favorite, and I think this is the last one, and we'll be right at 15 minutes, which will be perfect, is what I call the mind reading strategy. And this is how you can actually plant a thought in someone's mind. And it's by using a series of words. And the words are, you might be wondering. You might be thinking. And again, this translates, remember, copywriting is just salesmanship in print. Whether you're selling in person or you're selling in copy, same thing. It might be on your mind. Two things can happen here. One, they are thinking that. And so in essence, you have, quote, read their mind. But the second thing is, if they weren't thinking of that, the minute you say that, you've planted that thought in their head. So now they are thinking of it. So if you ever want to get people to think about something, you just simply use those phrases. You might be wondering. You might be thinking. It might be on your mind. And then it instantly is put into their mind, and they are thinking about it. If they're already thinking about it, you're in good shape, too. So let's give you an example of that. And here's one how this is one you can take and use immediately. You might be wondering, can I really get, so you've described all of the benefits that they're going to achieve, everything that they're going to achieve. So can I really get this benefit, this benefit, and this benefit, whatever the benefits that you just sold them? And then again, I use another embedded command, if I invest in this product or service today. So you might be wondering, can I really lose 10 pounds, three pan sizes, and have McDonald's every single day? Whatever it is. And so now, either they are wondering that, and we want them wondering that. But then we add in the embedded command of, if I invest in this product today, or if I invest in this service today. Everyone following that? OK. Any questions on this? Pretty straightforward stuff. Cool. So yeah. Yeah, bud? It goes without saying. Yes. Somebody has my question. So if you guys would like these slides, and I'm in the process of writing a report called How to Read Your Prospects Mind, if you guys want that, just send me an email at david.d.com. david.d.com. And in the subject line, this is really important. Put Dan Kennedy slides. Just put Dan Kennedy slides. And I'll send you the link.
